title: The Deterministic Telemetry Equation (Generator-Evaluator Decoupling Law) -- pre-registered BEFORE running
motivation: DeepMind's FunSearch / AlphaEvolve do not trust the LLM. They pair a PROBABILISTIC generator (the LLM, high capacity U, high variance, people-pleasing) with a DETERMINISTIC evaluator (a compiler / test suite / formal checker, zero false-accept). Hallucinations cannot survive the deterministic hop, so output fidelity is forced high by ARCHITECTURE, not by the generator's honesty. This is the deterministic complement to LISM's E = U * prod(D_i): the probabilistic system supplies U; the deterministic system supplies the final verification hop D_dec = 1.

equation.decoupling_law: F_out = F_eval,  and  d F_out / d F_gen = 0.  The output fidelity of a generate-and-filter pipeline equals the deterministic EVALUATOR's true-accept fidelity, and is INDEPENDENT of the generator's fidelity, provided the evaluator is deterministic with zero false-accept.
equation.yield: Y = U * s, where U = number of candidates the probabilistic generator emits and s = the deterministic survival (pass) fraction through the evaluator.
equation.pairing_principle: E = U * prod(D_i) requires BOTH systems. Probabilistic-only (generator is also verifier, i.e. chat/self-grading) => F_out = F_gen, which decays into people-pleasing noise. Deterministic-only => U = 0, no exploration. Neither alone discovers; the split does.

system_under_test: A fully DETERMINISTIC, offline FunSearch-style evolutionary loop: a seeded mutation GENERATOR (stands in for the LLM's variance) + a deterministic EVALUATOR (a hard-constrained objective function, here a 0/1 knapsack -- invalid candidates score -inf, exactly like a compiler rejecting invalid code). No network, no API, $0.

gates.D1_evaluator_determinism: The evaluator is deterministic: the SAME candidate scored repeatedly (>=3 repeats, and across a fresh process invocation) yields BIT-IDENTICAL scores (variance == 0). PASS iff exactly identical every time.
gates.D2_generator_variance: The probabilistic generator EXPLORES: across different seeds it produces genuinely different candidate populations (variance > 0). PASS iff distinct populations.
gates.D3_honesty_decoupling: Core claim. An HONEST generator and a LYING generator (identical proposals but the liar attaches an inflated self-reported score, RLHF-style) yield the EXACT SAME best solution under deterministic evaluation, because the evaluator discards self-reports. PASS iff |best_true(honest) - best_true(lying)| == 0 (exactly).
gates.D4_monotone_ratchet: best-so-far true score is monotone non-decreasing across generations (survivors only improve). PASS iff non-decreasing at every step.
gates.D5_architecture_control: The failure mode, as a control. A SELF-VERIFYING pipeline (trusts the generator's self-report, i.e. chat mode) run with the LYING generator selects a solution whose TRUE score is STRICTLY WORSE than the deterministic pipeline's (it is fooled by the inflated self-report). PASS iff true_quality(self_verify + liar) < true_quality(deterministic). This is why the deterministic hop is required.

symmetric_null: Report the measured outcomes AS-IS. D1 and D3 are EXACT predictions (variance 0, gap 0); if either is nonzero, that is a FAIL and must be reported, not rounded. D5 must show the self-verifying pipeline is genuinely worse; if it isn't, report that.
acceptance: GREEN when the spec re-hashes to the manifest, and D1-D5 all hold on the deterministic simulation (seeds fixed in the runner). No post-hoc gate changes.
open_source_grounding: The deterministic evaluator is the same KIND of object as a CI test suite, a compiler, or a formal checker on real open-source projects -- and as this repository's own hash-locked reproduce_all harness, which (documented in git history) caught a synthetic Kaggle CSV, a fabricated GILT script, and an attempt to spin the OpenAlex null into a false pass. That is real-world empirical evidence of the decoupling law: the deterministic evaluator rejected fabrications the probabilistic generator tried to pass.
honest_scope: This proves the ARCHITECTURAL law on a clean deterministic testbed (knapsack). It does NOT claim the LLM is honest, nor that any particular generator is good; it claims the SPLIT makes output fidelity independent of generator honesty. It complements, and does not replace, E = U * prod(D_i) and the tau_v enforcement-latency law.
